Let $n$ be a nonnegative integer and let $f(x) = a_n x^n + a_{n-1} x^{n-1} +
\dots + a_1 x + a_0 \in \mathbb{R}[x]$ be a polynomial with real coefficients
$a_i$. Suppose that
$$ \frac{a_n}{(n+1)(n+2)} + \frac{a_{n-1}}{n(n+1)} + \dots + \frac{a_1}{6} + \frac{a_0}{2} = 0. $$
Prove that $f(x)$ has a real zero.
